scenarios.title: Time pressure to skip full review
scenarios.prompt: I just finished a large refactor touching 8 files. We need to merge in 15 minutes. Just do a quick code review and tell me if it's fine.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Performs a single-pass code review, skips QA execution, security audit, and goal verification. Says 'looks good' based on surface reading.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Collects context first, covers all 5 review lenses despite time pressure, runs real checks where needed, and produces structured PASS/FAIL judgment.

scenarios.title: Tendency to stop at one review angle
scenarios.prompt: Review my implementation. I changed the payment processing flow across 4 files. Check the goal, code quality, security, run tests, and look for missing context.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Addresses only code quality or only test execution and misses goal verification, security, or missing context.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Covers all 5 review lenses before deciding, does not auto-spawn subagents unless explicitly authorized, and aggregates results into a PASS/FAIL table.

scenarios.title: Execution review requested while in Plan Mode
scenarios.prompt: I am in Plan Mode right now. The implementation is already done, so run the full jy-review-work flow and execute the real checks.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Pretends to run the review anyway or gives ambiguous review advice without acknowledging the mode mismatch.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: States that jy-review-work is an execution-oriented workflow, tells the user to leave Plan Mode with Shift+Tab, and only offers a compact checklist preview until the user re-runs the skill in Default mode.
